revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:封戸]]
{{Citation-needed|date=August 2009}} '''Fuko''' (封戸) refers to one aspect of the ancient [[:en:Japanese nobility|aristocratic]] {{ill|stipend|en|stipend|qid=Q10968259|lt=feudal stipend system|1=Stipend}} in Japan. It involved granting a certain number of households ([[:en:戸|戸]]) to aristocrats. The term "'''shikifu''' (食封)"<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%A3%9F%E5%B0%81-72689 Kotobank - "Shikifu"]</ref> refers to the actual system of granting these households, while '''fuko''' specifically denotes the households allocated as stipends.
In China, this system is analogous to '''shiyu''' (食邑) during the {{ill|Spring and Autumn period|en|Spring and Autumn period|qid=Q185047|lt=Spring and Autumn period|1=Spring and Autumn period}} and the {{ill|Han dynasty|en|Han dynasty|qid=Q7209|lt=Han dynasty|1=Han dynasty}}. It was systematized during the [[:en:Southern and Northern Dynasties|Southern and Northern Dynasties]].
== Japan == In Japan, fuko first appears in the [[Taika Reform]] Edict of {{ill|646|en|646|qid=Q33285|lt=646|1=646}}. It underwent changes after {{ill|676|en|676|qid=Q31098|lt=676|1=676}} (Hakuhō 5) and was later institutionalized in the {{ill|Taihō Code|en|Taihō Code|qid=Q1059892|lt=Taihō Code|1=Taihō Code}} and {{ill|Yōrō Code|en|Yōrō Code|qid=Q2575743|lt=Yōrō Code|1=Yōrō Code}}.
Various types of fuko existed: * '''[[Iifū]] (位封)''': Allocated based on Rank (to those of [[Junior Fifth Rank]] and above, later limited to [[Junior Third Rank]] and above after the [[Keiun Reform]]). * '''[[Shokufū]] (職封)''': Allocated based on official positions, granted to {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|qid=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon|1=Dainagon}} or higher (later {{ill|Sangi (Japan)|en|Sangi (Japan)|qid=Q1133663|lt=Sangi (Japan)|1=Sangi (Japan)}} or higher). * '''[[Kōfū]] (功封)''': Granted as a reward for notable achievements, given to individuals of Fifth Rank or higher. * '''[[Betchokufū]] (別勅封)''': Additional stipends personally granted by the emperor. * '''[[Ingyofū]] (院宮封)''': Allocated to institutions like the [[:en:Chūgū|Chūgū]] (Empress), {{ill|Daijō Tennō|en|Daijō Tennō|qid=Q14303973|lt=Daijō Tennō|1=Daijō Tennō}} (Retired Emperor), and the [[:en:Crown Prince|Crown Prince]].
For temples and shrines: * Temples were generally not eligible for fuko, though temporary allocations called '''[[Terafū]] (寺封)''' could be granted for up to five years. * Shrines were eligible for fuko in the form of '''[[Kamifū]] (神封)'''.
Under the [[Ritsuryō system]], provincial {{ill|Kokushi|en|Kokushi|qid=Q16801292|lt=Kokushi|1=Kokushi}} (国司) oversaw the collection of materials (封物) from fuko households. Half of the [[:en:so|so]] (租)<ref>Fuko stipends excluded the portion of rent intended for {{ill|Kokuga|en|Kokuga|qid=Q11418465|lt=Kokuga|1=Kokuga}} operations.</ref> and all of the {{ill|yō|en|yō|qid=Q9383473|lt=yō|1=Yō}} (庸) and {{ill|Chō|en|Chō|qid=Q1877216|lt=Chō|1=Chō}} (調) were delivered to the capital and distributed to the stipend holders.
As the [[public land and people system]] (公地公民制) of the Ritsuryō state declined, the transport of fuko resources to the capital ceased. In response, direct collection by stipend holders was permitted. Consequently, fuko lands often evolved into private estates ([[shōen]]), a transition closely linked to the development of [[:en:Feudal Japan|Feudal Japan]].
For example: * In {{ill|Bingo Province|en|Bingo Province|qid=Q1049113|lt=Bingo Province|1=Bingo Province}}, 50 households were allocated to {{ill|Daian-ji|en|Daian-ji|qid=Q1044009|lt=Daian-ji|1=Daian-ji}}, and 150 to [[Tōdai-ji]]. * Wooden tablets excavated from the [[Nagayao Residence]] reveal that Ashida District, Ashida Village, was part of the fuko granted to Empress {{ill|Empress Genshō|en|Empress Genshō|qid=Q235343|lt=Empress Genshō|1=Empress Genshō}} (formerly Princess Hidaka). * In {{ill|Aki Province|en|Aki Province|qid=Q965029|lt=Aki Province|1=Aki Province}}, [[Kōfuku-ji]] received 100 households, while Tōdai-ji received 50. These households delivered over 100 koku of rice annually.
== References == {{Reflist}}
